metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4784
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2, S2: high resolution imaging shows ANK3 (ankyrin-G) is localized to postsynapses, at both spine head and neck, in primary cultures of rat cortical neurons. Quantification of overlap between ANK3~Bassoon is similar to ANK3~PSD95 (Fig. S2G).

Authors observed; "To confirm presence of ankyrin-G at synapses, we immunostained for ankyrin-G and either the presynaptic marker bassoon (Figure 2C) or postsynaptic scaffold PSD95 (Figure 2D); both markers exhibited substantial colocalization with ankyrin-G (Figure S2D)"

Figure 4: the 190kDa short form of ANK3 is localized to spines. "Quantification of the fluorescence intensity of both isoforms in spines revealed 60% less spine localization of GFPAnkG270 compared with GFPAnkG190 and a reduced spine:shaft ratio (***p < 0.0002; Figures 4E and S4B), suggesting that the 190 kDa isoform is localized at synaptic sites in cortical neurons"

Figure 8: ANK3 (ankyrin-G) has greater association with both the cell membrane and cytoskeleton following a learning task in mice.. Experimental description: Supplemental M&M: "Dissociated cultures of primary cortical neurons were prepared from E18 Sprague-Dawley rat embryos."

antibodies:
Ankyrin-G mAb (Neuromab)
Ankyrin-G pAb (Santa Cruz)
Ankyrin-G pAb (from V. Bennett)

"GFP-Ankyrin-G-190 and RFP-Ankyrin-G-270 were purchased from Addgene (#31059, #42566). RNAi constructs were purchased from Origene, in the pGFP-V-RS vector with a turboGFP element to enable identification of transfected cells. The target sequence used was GGCAGAACGAGACGCCAAGTGGAAGCCTA"
